reasoning_model_1: Model 1 failed completely to generate the required speech content. Instead of speaking the text with a coughing tone, it generated a continuous, repetitive loop of coughing sound effects for the entire duration (0:00-0:18). There is no intelligible speech, resulting in a Content Accuracy score of 0. Consequently, it fails to meet the basic requirements of the prompt.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 performed excellent paralinguistic control. It correctly interpreted the text '咳咳' (Cough cough) as a non-verbal vocalization instruction. At 0:00-0:02, it produced realistic coughing sounds followed by a natural breath/throat-clearing sound ('uh/ah') before proceeding with the speech. The subsequent text '不好意思...' was delivered with high fluency, natural prosody, and correct content. The integration of the non-verbal sound with the speech was seamless and natural.
model_comparison: Model 1 is a complete failure as it produced no speech, only a loop of sound effects. Model 2 successfully generated the speech and accurately converted the specific text segments ('咳咳') into the corresponding non-verbal vocalization (coughing) as requested. Model 2 demonstrated high-quality control over paralinguistic features and natural speech generation.
